lesson.menu_desc: Describe places and attractions on a city tour with relative clauses (who, which, that) -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Sightseeing & Tours -- joining a tour, describing attractions, and using who / which / that to add information
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rubens Tofolo | Aula 19 -- Sightseeing & Tours

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone, and welcome to the city walking tour. My name is Sofia, and I will be your guide for the next two hours. Let me give you a quick overview of what we will see today. We will start at the old cathedral, which is the oldest building in the city. From there, we will walk to the central market, a bustling place that sells fresh fruit, spices, and local crafts. After the market, we will reach a scenic viewpoint that looks over the whole river. It is a spot which many photographers love. Our last stop is the art gallery, which has paintings from local artists. Admission to the gallery is included in your ticket. Please stay together, and if you have any questions, I am the person who can help. Let us begin.

Whenever I travel to a new city for a conference, I always keep one free morning for sightseeing. I am a doctor who works long hours, so a calm walk really helps me relax. I usually book a guided tour, because a good guide is someone who knows the hidden places that tourists never find alone. On my last trip, the guide took us to a small museum which was not in any guidebook. It had old medical instruments that fascinated me. After the tour, I like to buy something at a local market and sit at a scenic viewpoint that looks over the city. For me, sightseeing is the part of travel that I enjoy the most.

extra_audio.text: Here is how I enjoy a city tour. First, I find a guide who knows the city well. Then I join the group that meets near the cathedral. After that, we visit the attractions which are most famous. Next, I buy a ticket that includes admission to the gallery. Finally, I rest at a scenic viewpoint that looks over the river.
